intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: A dashboard with gentle, rounded corners and subtle shadows feels more approachable for personal health tracking than sharp, clinical layouts
facade.label: Soft rounded cards
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers welcoming, organic aesthetics over sterile or technical-looking interfaces
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer clean, minimal, or more structured/grid-based design approaches

evidence.hypothesis: A dashboard with gentle, rounded corners and subtle shadows feels more approachable for personal health tracking than sharp, clinical layouts
evidence.implication: User prefers welcoming, organic aesthetics over sterile or technical-looking interfaces

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer chronological visualization of health data over static snapshots
facade.axisTargeted: charts vs text — temporal progression representation
facade.label: Timeline strips
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into sequence-based layouts: scrollable timelines, progress bars, before/after comparisons; time dimension matters more than point-in-time metrics
facade.rejectImplies: Prioritize current state views: cards showing today's metrics, summary panels, status indicators; temporal narrative is secondary to present clarity

facade.hypothesis: Users building health tracking systems respond better to supportive, conversational language that celebrates progress rather than clinical, neutral language that just reports data
facade.label: Warm, encouraging tone
facade.acceptImplies: Voice should be warm, congratulatory, and personal—e.g., 'Great week of sleep!' instead of 'Sleep: 7.2h avg'
facade.rejectImplies: Voice should be clinical, neutral, data-focused—e.g., 'Sleep tracked: 7.2 hours average' with minimal personality

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid, eased motion between dashboard views over instant page changes
facade.label: Smooth sliding transitions
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace motion as a core design language; gestures and page slides should feel connected and gliding
facade.rejectImplies: Favor instant, click-based feedback; minimize animation; prioritize speed over visual flow

facade.hypothesis: The dashboard feels calmer and more intimate when using desaturated pastels and low-contrast palettes rather than bright or vivid tones
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool, calm vs energetic
facade.label: Soft, muted colors
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward serene, restful mood — likely prefers understated design that doesn't demand attention
facade.rejectImplies: User wants visual clarity and definition — may prefer higher contrast, bolder colors, or a more energetic feel

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar for experiment categories paired with scrollable card views for individual experiments will feel organized without overwhelming the dashboard
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation + Content Layout (sidebar vs. integrated tabs; cards vs. lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values clear category separation and modular content blocks; design should explore sidebar + card systems for health dashboards
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer flatter hierarchies, integrated tabs, or list-based layouts; sidebar might feel rigid or space-wasteful for this use case

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: A dashboard using warm color tones (amber, soft orange) and gentle inner glows creates a more inviting, less sterile atmosphere than cool grays and sharp contrasts
facade.label: Warm, glowing cards
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward warm, living color palettes and soft lighting effects that feel personal and comforting rather than clinical
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer cooler, crisper color schemes (blues, grays) or cleaner contrast hierarchies for clarity and focus in health tracking

draft.title: Experiment Tracker Dashboard
draft.summary: A personal health dashboard for monitoring long-term experiments with visual progress tracking, experiment cards, and key metrics at a glance.
